entry.title: Using PIRS, PMRP and MRP adopt time conflicts.
entry.content: Use separate planning horizons for PMRP and MRP, and allow for phase inconsistencies within the time periods. PMRP evaluates capacity for the current month, while MRP releases production recommendations based on the first working day of the month as the demand date.

entry.title: Ariba: In SCC-SBN how to send email notification to the Buyers to inform them of open approval item.
entry.content:
Hi Team,

How to send email notification to the Buyers to inform them of open approval item in Order Confirmation Deviation Approval.

Below screenshot is from the configuration guide. I am not able to understand clearly. It says we can add 5 emails under "Contact" element where exactly is this we need to configure?

What configuration needs to be done? or any CIG mapping required or any SR ticket needs to be raised?

In SBN-->Notification, I can configure upto 3 email address.(screenshot1)

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to remove a material extension from a...
entry.content: Hi Rashid, this is interesting, but it's not directly related to my question. Let me also clarify, we are using S4 Hana Cloud. To try to ask my question more plainly, if we maintain a distribution chain, which we later stop using because we start using common distribution channels, can we get the application Manage Product Master Data to ignore the old extension? Every time we try to save any piece of data in the application, we get a hard error saying we've maintained an invalid sales area. Thanks.

entry.title: Do service orders take into account common distribution channels and divisions?
entry.content:
We are using service orders in S4 public cloud.  When creating a service order for a non-stock good which is externally sourced, the expected action is it's supposed to trigger a purchase requisition.  However, it's getting an error saying that the material is not extended to the specified distribution channel.  It's worth noting, that the material is not specifically extended to the cited distribution channel, because we are using common distribution channels, so all materials should be extended to only one.  So this begs the question, can service orders work if you are maintaining common distribution channels?  

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano,

I am from Vietnam and still pursuing my studies, but I have a strong passion for ERP solutions and software development, particularly in the area of ABAP within SAP system.

As a Vietnamese student and a first-generation college attendee from my family, I aspire to contribute to the development of the IT field both domestically and internationally.

Becoming a professional ABAP developer is my goal for the future, and I believe that obtaining an SAP certification will help me enhance my skills and add greater value to the companies I will work for. I kindly ask you to consider my application for the SAP certification program.

entry.title: #sitMUC 2024: Last-Minute Details Inside
entry.content:
Get ready for an amazing day at #sitMUC 2024!
Find all the last-minute details you need right here:

Saturday 12 October 2024, from 09:00 to 18:00

Location:
SAP Labs Munich (MUE03)
Friedrich-Ludwig-Bauer-Straße 5, 85748 Garching bei München

Accessibility:

  • By U-Bahn: Line U6
    Get off at "Garching Forschungszentrum" station
    Walking directions from the U-Bahn to SAP (11min): OpenStreetMap
    (Following Google Maps is not recommended. Please see the OpenStreetMap image/link instead)
  • By Car: Free Parking available
    Onsite parking at the Labs building
    Additional spaces outside

Event Links: Community.sap.com  |  sitmuc.com/24

Agenda: Link

Looking forward to seeing you all there!

Cheers,
Your sitMUC Orga Team

entry.title: Re: BUSINESS PLACE IN SD DOCUMENT
entry.content: Can you please check Assign business places to plant for Goods and Services Tax for India (ID: 101316)?

entry.title: Re: What is the best ERP for a Startup?
entry.content:
Hi!

Some basic questions to start with - how big is your company in people size, what process do you want to cover? Basic finance? Procurement and Inventory mng? People management? Do you provide services and need billing? Or basic ability to issue invoice is enough?

i would start woth SAP S/4Hana Public Cloud ERP. SAP for GROW offering - it is more simple, friendly licensing and scalable. Start with basic finance functions and then gradually add more functions, processes.

entry.content: We helped you to accept the answer, since there were no updates after 7 days. You can unaccept it anytime if you have further questions.

entry.title: Re: Avoid hard coding time periods in SAC Planning data actions
entry.content:
Maybe you can try with something like this: 


MEMBERSET [d/Date] = PREVIOUS(9,"MONTH", %PERIOD_FORECAST%) TO %PERIOD_FORECAST%
MEMBERSET ...

VARIABLEMEMBER #PERIOD_VERSIE OF [d/Date]
VARIABLEMEMBER #PERIOD_VERSIE_VERGELIJK OF [d/Date]

DATA([d/Date] = #PERIOD_VERSIE) = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%)
DATA([d/Date] = #PERIOD_VERSIE_VERGELIJK) = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%)

DATA( [d/Date] = %PERIOD_FORECAST%) = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Date] = #PERIOD_VERSIE)/RESULTLOOKUP([d/Date] = #PERIOD_VERSIE_VERGELIJK)

entry.title: 月结后发现生产订单报工错误
entry.content:
各位专家好：

      目前遇到个问题，我们有个生产订单在8月错误报工并且订单已经完工收货，然后在10月才发现，导致该订单在8月月结后分摊了很大的成本在订单中，使成品物料的成本很大。现在要如何操作才能把那部分错误分摊的成本冲销或调整掉？

      PS：我们的物料账期MMPV已经开到了9月和10月

entry.title: Cannot release transport request in ABAP Cloud Project although I only receive ATC warnings
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I cannot release my transport request in ABAP Cloud Project although I only receive ATC warnings. Documentation says, that only errors have to be solved.

Does anyone know, what I must do to get my transport request released?

Thanks and regards, Sebastian

entry.title: Creating a popup with depending fields
entry.content:
Hi experts! 

I need to develop a Popup for a Fiori APP/serivce via REST in BTP, with two fields. Both fields need a value help or drop down list. The possible values list of field 2 are depending on the entered value in field 1. Both fields are mandatory, so I must garantee that either the field 2 can be maintained only if a value in field 1 is given or I need to display a messasge to inform the user that he has to maintain field 1 first. 

Regarding the message, I already created a Question - unfortunately without a usefull answer.

Display a message from Custom Entity / Query Imple... - SAP Community

Does anyone know, how to solve this basic reuqirement?

Thanks, Sebastian

entry.title: Re: Plant "phased" roll out of SAP S4/HANA for Product Co...
entry.content:
Hello Paulo

now to many topics are now "mixed" with each other. 

I try to exlpain step by step

1.) EHS Classic => Here you can do ALE between systems etc

2.) Product Compliance => currently no support is available for a SAP distributed landscape, The topic is on SAP Roadmap

3.) You can only acticate Product Compliance by "process". With 2023 i you can e.g. only activate "DG" but not the rest etc. and for the rest you can use EHS Classic in parallel (but you can not use PC DG and EHS Classic DG solution parallel).

BUT if you activate PC (for whatver subfunction") you must use PC (and you can not use EHS Classic) any more

The topic you are highlighting (plant code based roll out (or company code or similar object) is not supported and i can see no chance to get this

So the "3E Adpater" and the SAP solution can help to use EHS classic and PC in parallel

So we have to wait fo SAP; next on premise version will come roughly 2025; what we will get.. we will see..

C.B.

PS: Please check discussion running here: https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-q-a/s-4-hana-hybrid-connector-classic-vs-new-product-compliance-solution/qaq-p/13880661

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano,

       I hope this message finds you well. I first encountered SAP during my time in the United States Army, where we were transitioning from an outdated ERP system to GCSS-Army, which is based on SAP. Unfortunately, this transition occurred as I was leaving the military, so I didn't have much opportunity to work with SAP. However, I found the system to be fascinating.

     Currently, I work for the U.S. government, utilizing GCSS-Army primarily to pull reports and display data. While this has given me some exposure to the system, I am eager to deepen my understanding and engage with SAP beyond just data retrieval. I've been watching YouTube videos to learn more about its full capabilities, and I'm excited about the prospect of expanding my knowledge and skills in this area. As a disabled veteran, a first-generation college graduate, and a member of a minority group, I am particularly motivated to pursue this opportunity. Thank you for your time, and I look forward to any guidance you may have.

 

Best regards,

Alexander Conner

entry.title: About the default condition types of item in Sales order
entry.content:
When creating a sales order and looking at the price conditions of the material item, where does the default price type shown below come from and appear as shown below?

PPR0, YZWR, DCD2, PCIP

Please answer for me. 

Best regard.

entry.title: SOLMAN Test Suite Analytics: Overview and Access
entry.content:
SOLMAN Test Suite Analytics: Overview and Access

SAP Solution Manager’s Test Suite Analytics provides robust features for monitoring and analyzing testing activities. These analytics tools help manage test execution, track defects, and optimize test coverage, ensuring the quality of SAP systems. Below is a detailed summary of how to access these features and leverage them effectively.

How to Access Test Suite Analytics:

  1. Navigating to SAP Test Suite in SAP Solution Manager:

    • Launch the Fiori Launchpad from SAP Solution Manager.
    • Ensure that your user role has sufficient permissions to view test plans and analytics dashboards.

  2. Accessing Key Tabs and Tiles:

    • Tile: Test Suite – Overview
      • Navigate to the "Overview" tile to access high-level test status insights.
    • Tabs:
      • Test Planning Tab:
        View which test plans are ready for execution, monitor the status of test packages, and check for any open defects.
      • Test Execution Tab:
        Identify test packages assigned to you, planned start and end dates, and the status of assigned test cases.

Analytics Features and Reports:

  1. Completeness and Gap Reports:

    • Use this feature to determine which test cases are not yet assigned to a test plan, ensuring comprehensive coverage of all scenarios.

  2. Status and Progress Reports:

    • Track the real-time status of test plans and packages. These reports indicate:
      • Which test plans are in progress or completed.
      • The number of open defects associated with each plan or package.
      • Trends in defect resolution over time.

  3. Defect Analytics:

    • Defect Status and Priority:
      Analyze defects based on their current state (e.g., open, closed) and priority (high, medium, low).
    • Operation Test Effort Analytics:
      Measure the effort and time spent on resolving defects and compare it with historical data.

  4. Test Execution Analytics:

    • Monitor the number of test cases executed across testers and departments.
    • The dashboard provides metrics such as:
      • Test cases performed per tester.
      • Test cases in error without defects.
      • Active vs. passive testers and their contributions.

Using KPIs and Dashboards:

  1. Test Suite Dashboard:

    • Access the KPI Dashboard via the “Test Suite” tile.
    • Visualize key metrics such as:
      • Total number of resolved defects.
      • Number of defects per test plan, package, or test case.
      • Active testers vs. passive testers, helping optimize resource allocation.

  2. Test Case and Package Analytics:

    • Multiple Test Plan Status:
      Review status summaries for multiple test plans, including detailed analysis of individual test cases and packages.
    • Test Plans – Node and Test Case Changes:
      Monitor changes to nodes within a test plan to ensure no critical tests are omitted after modifications.

Generating Test Reports:

  • Test Plan Defect Analysis:
    Navigate to the relevant test plan to view detailed defect analytics and trends.
  • Generating Summary Test Reports:
    • Use the built-in reporting tools to create MS Word reports with:
      • Test plan details and status.
      • System landscape and solution documentation.
      • Lists of defects and their resolution statuses.
    • Reports are customizable with multiple selection parameters to fit project needs.

Conclusion:

SAP Solution Manager’s Test Suite Analytics provides essential tools for managing and optimizing testing processes. By accessing the Fiori Launchpad and using the appropriate tiles and dashboards, test managers and testers can monitor progress, manage defects, and ensure complete test coverage. With actionable insights from KPIs and comprehensive reports, teams can improve testing efficiency, minimize risks, and ensure smooth SAP deployments.

entry.title: Re: Starting out developing skills in analytics? Not sure where to start?
entry.content: "I've gained proficiency in SAC SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere, but I'm finding it overwhelming to delve into HANA, BW/4HANA, R, and JavaScript to effectively perform data analysis. Given my current skillset, could you provide guidance on the most valuable areas to focus on for achieving impactful results without feeling overwhelmed by the sheer number of technologies?" @MarkGreen

entry.title: Re: Sales tax to be applied as per ship to country
entry.content: Hi Anand , Have you found the solution to this problem ?

entry.title: Bill Of material (BOM)
entry.content:
Bill of Material (BOM) 

What is Bill of Material (BOM) and its role?

A bill of material is a complete, formally structured list of the components that make up a product or assembly. 

BOM can be single level and multi-level. For example, finished material would contain semi-finished materials as components which in turn would contain raw materials as components in next level.

Bill of Material having component number, description with quantity and unit measure.

There can be different alternative BOMs for a product depending upon the lot sizes, validity dates, and different production methods.

BOM is used in production planning for semi-finished and purchase planning for raw materials.

 

  • Use T code CS01 in SAP Easy Access

Enter Header Material and Plant, Select BOM Usage 1  Production

  • Enter the component that required to produce the product.

Quantity - Enter Material Quantity required

BOM Header

Tap on BOM Header to maintain the base quantity.

When we create more than 1 BOM of a product, it’s called Alternative BOM

When alternative BOM is more than 1 it’s called multiple BOM

BOM Status:

BOM Status should be 1 Active BOM which we ae going to use in transaction.

BOM Line Item

Select Item and select on item tab or double click on item.

Operation scrap refers to the expected material loss during a specific operation during production process.

When the material is a component, and is used as a part of an assembly and there is a loss when using that component. 

Recursive BOM can be understood as the material that uses the same header material for its make. You can also take an example of Curd we need curd to make curd so this is perfect example of Recursive BOM.

BOM Configuration:

1.Define default value [ Base Quantity Header and BOM Status]

To define default base quantity of header and default BOM status

 

  1. Determine the BOM Explosion mode

To define default Explosion Mode for BOM Header

Enable BOM Validity calculation

 2. Define BOM Usage

Define the BOM usage as per user requirement in which area use want to use

3. Define BOM Status

BOM status define for BOM availability for Transaction in various area like costing, plan order, Sales order etc.

  1. Define Allowed material type for BOM item

 To define Material type which we are going to use for BOM item in BOM usage 1 with respect to BOM header

BOM Like: FERT, HALB ETC.

BOM like: ROH, HALB RTC.

[ + Allowed, - Not Allowed]

BOM Reporting

T codes

CS11: Level by Level BOM Explosion

CS12: Multi Level BOM Explosion

CS13: Summarized BOM

CS14: BOM Comparison

CS15 : Where used material List

 

  • There might be the case wherein component material does not exist. For this, you need to create the material master for the component material before creating BOM.
  • Some users try to create the BOM again for the same Product, system gives warning message regarding “Alternative BOM2 is being created” and they tend to ignore the warning message and move forward and hence end up in creating similar alternative BOM 2 for same product. This unnecessary creates duplicate data.

Thankyou.

entry.title: Re: Non-archiving solution for GRACAUDITLOG data cleanup in SAP GRC
entry.content:
Thanks Mate for your feedback, it's an alternate solution to clean up the EAM data as per retention policy and depends on customers whether they use this program or archiving solution. Also, we need to follow SAP recommendation and that suggested by you as well. Thanks

Regards,

Shiv

entry.title: How to connect to Analytic Model in Power BI
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I am importing a data source in SAP Datasphere with ODBC connector and using it in PBI.

I tried to use Analytic Model from datasphere in PBI.

But calculated and restricted measures created in Analytic Model are not displayed.

Only the fact tables are imported.

 

Is there a way to connect and use the Analytic Model in PBI?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano  
I appreciate SAP's commitment to supporting cloud learning and the Digital Skills Initiative.

entry.title: PPDS on SAP GUI for Java known issues
entry.content:
Hy guys,

I am currently using a Mac M3 with sap gui for Java 7.8. At the moment I am trying to learn PPDS and have noticed i can’t open CDPS0 on gui because it can’t execute „Control“ because its only supported for windows. Are there any other known issues regarding PPDS and sap GUI for Java?

 

I already asked our IT but they won’t support installing a windows VM and doing it by myself would be against company regulations. Has anyone found a workaround to this issue? I really would like to keep the Mac, but if it’s not possible i guess I have to ask for a windows notebook again. Using the fioris is not an option because I have been told/ shown that it doesn’t have the same functions as in GUI.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: maintenance plan fixed call date on last day of every...
entry.content:
Hey there,
The solution is already provided here:
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/maintenance-plan-scheduling-by-key-date/qaq-p/703203

I've checked it myself, and yes - it works.

What u gotta do in a nutshell in the Maintenance Plan setup:
1. Cycle and unit: 1 DAY
2. Scheduling indicator: Factory Calendar
3. Factory Calendar: AM
4. Scheduling period: doesn't matter, go with 3M if u want 3 month preview
5. Start of cycle: end of the month u select

Cheers,
Dan

entry.title: Re: Consuming a REST Service with the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model
entry.content:
Hi 

I have a similar requirement where I need to convert solr response into odata using a java service. I have the actual response which are search results and some metadata. The problem is, the main search filters are part of $expand($filter) as the main entity is contained in a wrapper. I have 2 doubts related to this.

1) How can I set metadata tags like nextLink manually in odata response ?

2) How can I read $filter inside $expand ?

 

I am using sap cap java. 

Regards

Sparsh

entry.title: Re: PPDS on SAP GUI for Java known issues
entry.content: https://me.sap.com/notes/0002616384

entry.title: Re: PPDS on SAP GUI for Java known issues
entry.content: This is the SAP Note regarding that issue https://me.sap.com/notes/0002616384

entry.title: My intro
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,
My name is Faith and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about myself: I’m originally from NJ but I am based out in TX. I graduated from Rutgers University with a bachelor’s degree in information systems, in 2020. I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge in business processes and implementation and regulations.
I’m particularly excited about obtaining this certification because it will help my career path in becoming a GRC analyst & expand my knowledge in other areas of IT. I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in cybersecurity or the GRC side of cybersecurity. Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!
Best regards,

entry.content: Insightful

entry.title: Re: LMS User connector SF getting failed
entry.content: @BS181, it's possible to review if an FTP/SFTP is being used to place the file and if the file is fully available in the FTP/SFTP when Learning is scheduled to run (Export Cautions for User Connector - SF).

These are the percentage selection, the upload percentages and the defined task,

 

Please, could you help me?

Thanks,

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Quering an OData Endpoint with a timestamp does not w...
entry.content:
Hi @jensniemeyer ,

Have you tried the SQL-like fluent API mentioned in the documentation?

I'm thinking something like this:

 

SELECT(["AccountID", "ContactID"]).from("ContactCollection").where({ ChangedOn: { '>': myDate } })

 

Could you check if this could work?

Rgards,

Peter

entry.title: Re: The value 'null' cannot be set as 'From' address in t...
entry.content:
Hi @rautrkai ,

It's not entirely clear to me, but from the error message and your description, I think the solution might be to make sure that the value in From is a valid email, but at least not empty or null, before calling the send email routine.

I hope this helps.

Regards,

Peter

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP CDS Distinct does not return distinct values
entry.content: Hi, Could you write a simple example of what data should be returned in DistinctUsers for a set of SAPUsers (userId, email, userName)?

entry.title: Where do I get the Course Files (zip file) for practice on the system (HandsOn THR81)?
entry.content:

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear David,

Thank you for the opportunity that SAP is providing to participate in SAP Learning’s Digital Skills Initiative!

I am having more than 10+ years of professional experience in SAP ERP and now I would like to enhance my career opportunities becoming a Certified SAP Consultant.

My learning Journey starts from the course SAP Data Analyst – SAP Analytics Cloud and to complete Certification I have to pass next exams:

SAP Certified Associate - Data Analyst - SAP Analytics Cloud

SAP Certified Application Associate - SAP Business One Release 10.0

SAP Certified Specialist - SAP Activate for Cloud Solutions Project Manager.

I would be very thankful for you to attend Certification Exams using the possibility provided by Digital Skills Initiative.

Thank you!

Best Regards,

Lidiia Koltsova

Also want to know the date for WE20 entry creation.

Regards

Zunaid Hingora.

entry.title: Re: Certify Your Skills as SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Consultant
entry.content:
Hi Dieter,

It's absolutely clear for me. If SAP as ERP solution provider or the other companies that need to be implemented SAP Public Cloud requiring SAP Public Cloud certification as tender material consider that the acknowledgment of Public Cloud certification for other LoBs only uses certification of C_S4CPB plus RoA as your explanation, then we will follow the update information. However I think the best way for SAP Public Cloud certification that SAP should provide for each LoB as available solution currently, such as Asset Management, Manufacturing, Professional Services, etc. Because C_S4CPB may be too general for the other LoBs.

Thanks for your response beforehand.

entry.title: To create a new wage for deduction of HRR which is not considered for housing perk calculation.
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am looking your help regarding a query. please go through the following issue received from client and let me know how to do. if possible please guide me with screen shots, it will be helpful to me as future reference.

QUERY:  

In respect of employee no. xxxxx and by maintaining wage type 2054 for deduction of HRR of Rs. 3040 p.m.., Housing Perks is not required .
However, by maintaining wage type 2054 in other case, Housing Perks is ok even system is also fine.
In view of above, it is requested to kindly create new sub wage type for deduction of HRR in case of CVO only so that the correct pay slip ( Gross - Deductions ) may be issued.
Now the client requesting to create a new wage for deduction of HRR which is not considered for housing perk calculation.
 
Regards...
Ananthj

My name is Samer Alhato. Working as HR analyst using SAP &SF system. I'm new on this group and would like to inquire how can get the maximum befits of such group on SAP & SuccessFactors on topic or events related to Middle east.

Hope if anyone can assist me here.

below is my email

samer.alhato@tronox.com

entry.title: Re: Extensibility for Manage Prices - Sales
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

We have a requirement to trigger the workflow based on pricing communication data. We created the workflow and implemented Badi SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON but it doesn't have pricing communication as importing and even standard CDS available for pricing will not return the pricing data during first time create of sales order as it is not created yet. This blog says about the data to be transferred from Order Item to Pricing, however is there any business process that would pass on the data from pricing communication to item of the order. Just FYI, order item has subtotal fields available to aggregate the values from the pricing procedure but all of them are occupied. Unfortunately, I couldn't find any solution for this yet. Have anyone came across such requirement and able to crack, request your inputs on it.

Best,

HP

entry.title: Release strategy for Purchase Order not working
entry.content:
Hello expert,
I've been trying to implement a Purchase Order Release Strategy to see if it works. It should trigger when INCO1 = CFR and PO Type = It is not working.

Can you please help me out? I'll attach my screens.

 

Thanks so much in advance!

entry.title: Requirement to trigger the workflow based on pricing communication data during Sales Order Create
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

We have a requirement to trigger the workflow based on pricing communication data. We created the workflow and implemented Badi SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON but it doesn't have pricing communication as importing and even standard CDS available for pricing will not return the pricing data during first time create of sales order as it is not created yet. There are blog that says about the data to be transferred from Order Item to Pricing, however is there any business process that would pass on the data from pricing communication to item of the order. Just FYI, order item has subtotal fields available to aggregate the values from the pricing procedure but all of them are occupied. Unfortunately, I couldn't find any solution for this yet. Have anyone came across such requirement and able to crack, request your inputs on it.

Best,

HP

entry.title: Guidance on Transitioning from SAP Business One Consultant to SAP S/4HANA FICO consultant
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have 10 years of experience working with SAP Business One and am now transitioning to become an SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud FICO consultant. Could you kindly guide me on how to correlate the key differences between SAP Business One and S/4HANA, particularly in terms of functionality, additional features, and configuration tasks in FICO?

Furthermore, if there are any relevant documents or study materials available, I would greatly appreciate it if you could share them.

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
Silambu

entry.title: Connecting to a RAC Database in SAP Integration Suite: A Step-by-Step Guide Using JDBC Adapter
entry.content:
Connecting to a Cluster Database (RAC) in Suite: A Challenge and Solution

The limitations of direct connections to RAC databases in Suite by leveraging HTTP calls through PI to CPI. This innovative approach provides a flexible and reliable solution for integrating with clustered databases.

Prerequisites:

  • Cluster Database (RAC): Ensure you have a properly configured RAC database instance running.
  • SAP Integration Suite: Have SAP Integration Suite installed and configured.
  • PI (Process Integration): Have PI installed and configured to communicate with your RAC database.
  • CPI (Cloud Platform Integration): Have CPI set up and configured to interact with PI and the RAC database.
  • HTTP Client: You'll need an HTTP client tool (e.g., Postman, curl) to make the necessary HTTP calls.

Process:

IFlow:

Fetch the table data: Content modifier property details

Content Modifier Message Body

 ${property.Http_Select}

 

Http_Select: REST calls support both JSON and XML, so the HTTP input format for this integration should be XML. The table and query information should be included in the REST input as XML elements.

The SELECT query should be defined in the access element, while the table name should be specified in the action element.

<root>
<stmt>
<TableName action="SQL_QUERY">
<access>Select fieldnames from tablename and rownum = 1</access>
</TableName>
</stmt>
</root>

Http Adapter:

HttpAddress: http://<PIHost>:<PIPort>/RESTAdapter/Call_PI/Send_JDBC

To establish a connection between the Cloud Connector and the on-premises PI system, the following information must be configured:

  • PI Host and Port: These details are essential for the Cloud Connector to locate and communicate with the on-premises PI system.
  • Location ID and PI Credentials: These credentials are required to authenticate and authorize the Cloud Connector's access to the PI system.
  • REST Call Address: The same HTTP address should be used in the REST call within PI to ensure proper communication between the two systems.

Pass through ICO in PI:

REST Call:

JDBC Call: A service-based connection to an Oracle DB running on Oracle RAC (cluster).

Groovy Script: replace root node

To align the structure of the CPI response with the JDBC response.

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import java.util.HashMap;
def Message processData(Message message) {
//Read Input as String
def body = message.getBody(String);

//Replace XML Root and Sub Node
String output = body.replace("root_response", "ROOT").replace("stmt_response", "select_response");

//Set Output
message.setBody(output);
return message;
}

Payload difference before and after groovy

Before groovy:

After groovy:

Following the Groovy script, the integration flow can proceed to the next step based on the script's output and the defined requirements.

NOTE:SAP has recommended the Edge Integration Cell as the ideal solution for this requirement within SAP Integration Suite. However, due to our tight timeline, we're exploring a more immediate option using SAP PO, which already has established connections.

Bhavani.

entry.title: How to call a CPI Artifact (REST / SOAP API) from SAP API Management
entry.content:
Pre requisites for this blog

  • Activate - Manage API capability (if not activated yet)
  • Setup API Business Hub Enterprise and add your user as Developer

Introduction

In this blog, I am going to use a public calculator SOAP service as an example and create a SOAP API as CPI artifact then call the same SOAP API from SAP API Management. So, let's begin.  

Step # 1: Create a SOAP API

  • Create a new package or edit an existing one (I am creating a new package with name "Calculator")
  • Add a new artifact, choose API as artifact type.

  • A popup window will appear, asking for the runtime profile information choose Cloud Integration and click Next
  • On the next screen, select "SOAP API" as API type and click Next
  • Choose New and click Next
  • Give API an name (in my case, i name it as "Calculator API" ) and click Create

A new SOAP API will be created inside the package, open the API in edit mode and delete Content Modifier from Handle Request - Local Integration Process. 

 

Step # 2: Edit the Calculator API 

Note: I am going to use dneonline (open the link and save the WSDL in your local drive for later use)web service in this example.

  • edit the "SOAP" as sender adapter setting
    • give an address to the SOAP API 
    • select WSDL in "Service Definition" and browse the WSDL you have save earlier. Once you select the WSDL choose operation you want to perform.

  • Add "Request-Reply" step in Internal integration process and connect it with receiver and choose "SOAP" as receiver adapter type.

  • Open SOAP setting (as receiver adapter) and edit as below

  • Save and Deploy the SOAP API

 Step # 3: Test SOAP API

Once API is deployed, download the WSDL definition from Monitor > Integrations & APIs. Go to SoapUI and browse the newly saved WSDL definition. Choose Add operation and test the service. (Note: for authentication use integration-flow type service key credentials) 

Step # 4: Create a Provider as Type "Cloud Integrations"

Note: Copy the "API" type service key information (open BTP Cockpit and go to subscription page there locate "Instances" table, download/copy the key information.

First, we need to create an API Provider for that: 

  • go-to "Configure >> APIs" 
  • then go-to "API Providers" tab
  • and click "Create" 
  • Type name as "CPI Provider" and move to next tab "Connection"
  • Select "Cloud Integrations"
  • Open the downloaded key file and copy the URL 
  • Paste it in "Cloud Integration Management Host" field
  • select Authentication type as "OAuth2ClientCredentials"
  • Copy ClientID and Client-Secret and paste respectively.
  • Save and Test the connection

Step # 5: Create a API Proxy using CPI Provider

  • Go to Configure > APIs, this time select "API Proxies" tab
  • Click "Create"
  • A new popup window will appears, select API Provider in radio list
  • and choose the newly created CPI Provider from the drop-down list and click "Discover"
  • A new dialog will open, where you will find the Calculator API we created in Step 1, choose it and click "Next"
  • For authentication choose "Basic" and input the credentials which you have use to test the SOAP API in step 3 and click "Done"
  • Once you back to the API Create window you will see few fields are already filled in, you can change the text if wish to but keep the URL value as it is, i am keeping them as it is. 

  • Click "Create" to create the Proxy
  • On the next screen, click "Save" and then "Deploy".

  • Copy the API Proxy URL 

Step # 6: Test the API Proxy

  • Open SoapUI and edit the endpoint on the request we testing in Step 3
  • set the authentication to "No Authentication" and click "Submit" 

Step # 7: Apply Policy to Secure the API

  • Edit the API Proxy created step 5
  • and go to Policies 
  • On the next screen select "Edit"
  • On the left hand side Policy Edit expand the "Proxy Endpoint" and select "PreFlow"
  • Then choose Verify API Key policy (click plus sign) from right hand side of the side menu 

  • Give a name to the policy and click "Add"

  • update the variable reference name to  "request.header.apikey"

  • click "Update" button 
  • and then Save and Deploy again

Step # 8: Create a Product and Subscribe it

  • To create a product go to Engage 

  • Keep on Products Tab and click "Create"
  • Type name and title and move to "APIs" tab
  • Click "Add" and select the Calculator API Proxy from the list and Click OK
  • Publish the Product
  • Now go to API Business Hub Enterprise 

  • You will see the product you created earlier there
  • select the product and go to next page
  • To subscribe choose New application from the drop-down list
  • Give a name and click Create 

  • Now, copy the key for testing 

Step #9: Test API with API Key

  • Go to SoapUI again
  • add a header as "apikey" and paste the copied key in value field 
  • now submit the request you will receive the response 

Conclusion

In this way, you can secure your CPI artifacts from direct access. SAP API Management offers lot of different policies which help developer built a security layer around the backend services. I just added the authentication mechanism, but it offers much more such as Traffic Management Policies, Security Management Policies, Mediation Policies.

entry.title: Feedback about SAP TechEd 24 "portal" - Virtual registration
entry.content:
Hi mates,

I would like to thanks SAP, sponsors and partner for achieving the release of the SAP TechEd 24 virtual edition.
The portal with dashboard is really great, especially the search engine and the "tag" feature to browse through contents with a "product" oriented view and track type !

Thanks

Amaury

entry.title: Re: trace all queries coming for specific database
entry.content:
Hi,

Please check the below and see which one is feasible to you in your case.

1.Using MDA tables 
   select * from monProcessSQLText
   select * from monSysSQLText

2. You can enable auditing to capture SQL text by turning it on to for each login/object you want to monitor. Please see the documentation under www.help.sap.com for auditing.

3. Using dbcc commands for SQL text and from sp_w/sp_who/master..sysprocesses for the info.
dbcc traceon (3604)
dbcc sqltext(<spid>)

Regards,
Dilip Voora

entry.title: Re: Fiori attachments storage into OpenText archive
entry.content:
Hello,

there are indeed more and more Fiori Apps that are storing attachments as DMS objects.

This means that DIRs (Document Info Record) are automatically created by the system and are:

- On one hand connected to the object on which you are adding attachments

- On the other hand storing the attachment

You can use CV04N to retrieve the list of created DIRs. There if you open one at the bottom of the first screen you'll see the attachments. The important element there, to find out where they are physically stored, is the Storage Cat. attribute = Storage Category.

Once you have identified the Storage Category, just go in transaction OACT to retrieve it; there you can also see the corresponding content repository, define in OAC0 and that can be database storage, OpenText content server etc. 

In OACT you can change the content repository so that the Storage Category used in the DIRs is well going to your desired content server.

In DMS configuration you can also do the changes to use another storage category...

Rgds

entry.title: Re: Store attachments from Standard Fiori apps in Archive...
entry.content:
Hi,

Indeed HDM is restricted to few Standard Fiori apps only. SAP shall very probably add more and more Apps over the next releases but of course we don't know how many releases it will take before most of it is covered.

Also note that some Apps, and Dispute Management is one of them, are storing attachments yet in a slighlty different way technically.

entry.title: Re: Vendor notes and Attachments data migration from ECC...
entry.content:
Simplest is to use some Z programs.

One on the ERP will extract all attachments and store them on a file system keeping the file name and the object to which they are connected in the ERP.

The other imports all files in S/4 adding attachments directly to the corresponding objects. If the object keys are different you should use a mapping table.

This is something I have often done in various environments.

Other possibility is to run the Z program on S/4 only and allow RFC connection to the ERP in order to extract and transfer attachments without going through a temporary file system. This requires however that RFC connection from S/4 to ERP is possible and allowed. Can be a bit more challenging if S/4 is in the cloud...

entry.title: Re: How to map the Archive server to Kpro in SAP S4 DMS
entry.content:
In DMS configuration, check the Kpro 'on'

In OACT change the storage category to make it point to the content repository you want to use. The default storage category is also set in the DMS configuration.

entry.title: Re: About the default condition types of item in Sales order
entry.content: Hi Jason, Please check the pricing procedure assigned to Sales area/ customer pricing procedure/ Document pricing procedure ... all the seen condition types will be assigned in that .. for corresponding values, either check material master or pricing condition records maintained. .. thank you

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano & Digital Skill Initiative Team,

Thank you for this opportunity!

I am having more than 8+ years of professional experience in Modeling and Reporting and now I would like to get SAP Analytics Cloud Certification. 

I am a woman, single parent and one of the people looking to switch their careers mid-life.

I would be very thankful for you if you let me have a Certification Exam Attempt provided by Digital Skills Initiative.

I am looking forward for your support and a certification attempt.

Best Regards,
